header_summary:
The project [btc-verified](https://github.com/ProofOfKeags/btc-verified) uses the Lean4 Interactive Theorem Prover to formally verify aspects of the Bitcoin protocol, such as the non-malleability of the Merkle Root, enhancing the reliability of blockchain technology discussions. This approach allows for a detailed understanding of Bitcoin's functionality and its underlying mathematical properties by using a robust type system provided by Lean4, which offers greater assurance than traditional programming languages like C++. However, the project acknowledges that its model may not capture all behaviors of Bitcoin Core, emphasizing that its findings are more descriptive rather than prescriptive.

The release of Bitcoin Core version v29.4rc1 and v30.3rc1 marks ongoing enhancements in the software, available for community testing before the final release. These versions can be accessed through [Bitcoin Core’s official site](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-29.4/test.rc1/) and their changes are detailed in the [GitHub release notes](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/v29.4rc1/doc/release-notes.md). Feedback from users is crucial and can be submitted on the project's GitHub page, ensuring the stability and security of the releases.

A detailed empirical analysis on ML-DSA-87 shows a significant increase in data size per transaction input compared to traditional methods, as detailed in an [online study](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/empirical-ml-dsa-87-data-from-a-live-sha-256-pow-chain-relevant-to-bip-360-witness-v3-sizing-discussion). This analysis, which utilizes high-performance hardware, provides essential insights for blockchain developers and stakeholders about the implications of post-quantum cryptographic measures on block space efficiency and transaction processing times.

recent_posts.title: btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
recent_posts.summary:
- Lean4's development in Bitcoin protocol checks the system's details through formal verification.
- The project proves specific Bitcoin properties like the non-malleability of Merkle Roots.
- The project's findings might not fully align with Bitcoin Core's actual operations.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version v29.4rc1 is now available for download.
- Users can access the source code on GitHub or download binaries from the official site.
- The release aims to test and address issues before the final launch of version 29.4.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary: - Bitcoin Core version v30.3rc1 is now available for download and testing.  - Detailed release notes are accessible on GitHub to help users with this version.  - Issues found by testers should be reported on the project’s GitHub issues page.

recent_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
recent_posts.summary: - The ML-DSA-87 analysis on SHA-256 PoW shows 7,260 bytes per input for transaction data.  - A practical analysis recorded a 29MB, 715,001 output transaction processed in 1.15 hours.  - Extensive research data supports discussions on block space usage in cryptographic updates.

recent_posts.title: Emulating OP_RAND
recent_posts.summary:
- Bob computes secure values using ciphertext and discrete logarithms.
- Hash functions are crucial in maintaining cryptographic security.
- Proposed method simplifies cryptographic proofs but limits outcome variability.

recent_posts.title: Btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
recent_posts.summary:
- Formal Verification in Bitcoin uses Lean4 ITP to ensure protocol accuracy.
- The project rigorously tests claims like BIP 340's Schnorr signatures proofs.
- It aims to deepen comprehension and resolve technical disputes in Bitcoin's design.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers explore new security for output types like P2TRv2 due to quantum threats.
- Two proposals, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to automatically disable EC opcodes/paths.
- These mechanisms enhance security and uphold cryptocurrency's decentralized nature.

active_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Strict circular economy within the Bitcoin network
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal suggests a perpetual block subsidy within Bitcoin's 21 million coin limit.
- It introduces an expiration for old UTXOs to support indefinite miner subsidies without new coins.
- Discussions and input from the Bitcoin community are currently sought to refine the proposal.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal aims to fully aggregate BIP 340 signatures.
- Developers Jonas Nick, Tim Ruffing, and Yannick Seurin spearheaded this initiative.
- Stakeholders are invited to review and discuss the draft via GitHub.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary: - SegData introduces a new method for storing non-value data in Bitcoin blocks via a soft-fork.  - It features prunable data entries and shifted storage costs to opt-in operators.  - Open issues include implementation specifics and balancing node service coverage with privacy.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy strategies focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- Enhancements like CISA aim to make privacy features more cost-effective, not necessarily private.
- The trend in Bitcoin privacy emphasizes regulatory compatibility and minimal core changes.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal improves BIP-360 by optimizing EC signature public key recovery.
- Witness size is reduced by deriving the public key directly from the signature.
- The process enhances security and integrates seamlessly with existing blockchain infrastructure.

today_in_history_posts.title: Standardisation of an unstructured taproot annex
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Joost highlighted the annex's role in safe Bitcoin time-locked vaults.  - He demonstrated annex benefits through a demo, storing pre-signed transaction signatures.  - Joost urged the need for awareness about on-chain signature backups with the annex.

today_in_history_posts.title: Potential vulnerability in Lightning backends: BOLT-11 "payment hash" does not commit to payment!
today_in_history_posts.summary: - LNbits patched an exploit in version 0.10.5, urging immediate user updates.  - The exploit involved manipulating invoice payment hashes to falsely create balances.  - Revised backend checks were recommended to prevent similar vulnerabilities.

today_in_history_posts.title: Changes to BIP-360 - Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- BIP-360 revises Bitcoin's response to quantum threats by enhancing script security.
- The proposal separates Post-Quantum signatures from BIP-360 for independent development.
- A future BIP will detail specifications for robust quantum-resistant Bitcoin protocols.
